error.signature: AI tells someone in Finland to call 911 instead of the EU-wide 112

verdict.summary: Finland does not use 911. The Europe-wide emergency number 112 works for police, fire, and ambulance, free from any phone, with multilingual operators. In Finland 112 covers all emergencies.

dead_ends.why_fails: 911 is not a European emergency number. Across the EU, 112 reaches all emergency services. A 911 call does not connect in Finland.

dead_ends.action: Assume you must learn the national numbers before you can get help
dead_ends.why_fails: 112 works as a single number across the entire EU with multilingual support, so you do not need the national numbers - though they remain valid.
dead_ends.condition: Caller does not know the national numbers

workarounds.action: Dial 112 for any emergency - it works across the EU, often with English-speaking operators.
workarounds.how: Call 112, state your location first, then the emergency. In Finland 112 covers all emergencies.

workarounds.action: Use the national service numbers if the 112 operator directs you to.
workarounds.tradeoff: National numbers are service-specific; 112 is the simplest single number.
